popularStory.headline: Christopher Browne, MP 1450, Agnes Bedingfield 1485
popularStory.description:
CHRISTOPHER BROWNE, MP  1450 - 1516
AGNES BEDINGFIELD  1485 - 1579

Christopher was born between 1450 and 1457 in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England.  His parents were Anne Browne and John Brown, IV. …

zh.lifeSummary: Christopher Browne I是John Browne IV和Agnes Unknown的兒子，於1457年5月1日出生在英國林可爾史爾斯坦福。他於1474年與Grace Pinchbeck在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾斯坦福結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世在英國英格蘭素福爾可斯萬斯 霍爾，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可霍可頓。

nameMeanings.name: Browne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Brown
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

nameMeanings.name: Christopher
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Khristophoros, from Khristos ‘Christ’ + pherein ‘to bear’. This was popular among early Christians, conscious of the fact that they were metaphorically bearing Christ in their hearts. A later, over-literal interpretation of the name gave rise to the legend of a saint who actually bore the Christ-child over a stream; he is regarded as the patron of travellers. In England the name was uncommon in the Middle Ages, but became very popular in the 16th century, especially in parts of the North.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Christopher Browne I was born on 1 May 1457, in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England as the son of John Browne IV and Agnes Unknown. He married Grace Pinchbeck in 1474, in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in Swans Hall, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Hawkedon, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Christopher Browne I est né le 1 mai 1457, à Stamford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, fils de John Browne IV et Agnes Unknown. Il a épousé Grace Pinchbeck en 1474, à Stamford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé à Swans Hall, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Hawkedon, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Christopher Browne Iは1457年5月1日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスタムフォードでJohn Browne IVとAgnes Unknownの息子として生まれた。1474年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスタムフォードでGrace Pinchbeckと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼はイギリスイギリススフォルクスワンス ハルで死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクハウケドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Christopher Browne I(은)는 1457년 5월 1일 영국 링코실르 스타퍼드에서 John Browne IV(와)과 Agnes Unknown의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1474년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 스타퍼드에서 Grace Pinchbeck(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 스완스 홀에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 호케던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Christopher Browne I nasceu em 1 de maio de 1457, em Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra filho de John Browne IV e Agnes Unknown. Ele casou-se com Grace Pinchbeck em 1474, em Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em Swans Hall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Hawkedon, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Christopher Browne I родился 1 мая 1457 г. в местности стамфорд, линколншир, Англия. Его родителей звали John Browne IV и Agnes Unknown. Он женился на женщине по имени Grace Pinchbeck в 1474 г. в местности стамфорд, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в местности сванс холл, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности ховкедон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.
